--- a/parsl/monitoring/visualization/views.py
+++ b/parsl/monitoring/visualization/views.py
@@ -1,6 +1,7 @@
 import pandas as pd
 from flask import Blueprint, jsonify, request
 from sqlalchemy import text
+from sqlalchemy.orm import Session
 
 from parsl.monitoring.db_manager import DBManager
 
@@ -15,13 +16,14 @@ def dag_group_by_states(workflow_id):
     """
     db = DBManager()
     engine = db.engine
-    
-    query = """SELECT task.task_id, task.task_func_name, task.task_depends, status.task_status_name
+    
+    query = """SELECT task.task_id, task.task_func_name, task.task_depends, status.task_status_name
                FROM task LEFT JOIN status
                ON task.task_id = status.task_id
                AND task.run_id = status.run_id
                AND status.timestamp = (SELECT MAX(status.timestamp)
                                        FROM status
                                        WHERE status.task_id = task.task_id and status.run_id = task.run_id
                                       )
-               WHERE task.run_id='%s'""" % (workflow_id)
-    df = pd.read_sql_query(query, engine)
+               WHERE task.run_id=:run_id"""
+    df = pd.read_sql_query(text(query), engine, params={"run_id": workflow_id})
     
     # ... rest of the function
@@ -45,10 +47,11 @@ def get_task_tries(workflow_id):
     db = DBManager()
     engine = db.engine
     
-    df_task_tries = pd.read_sql_query("""SELECT ...
-                                     WHERE task.task_id = try.task_id AND task.run_id='%s' and try.run_id='%s'"""
-                                     % (workflow_id, workflow_id), db.engine)
+    query = """SELECT ...
+               WHERE task.task_id = try.task_id AND task.run_id=:run_id and try.run_id=:run_id"""
+    df_task_tries = pd.read_sql_query(
+        text(query), 
+        engine, 
+        params={"run_id": workflow_id}
+    )
     
     # ... rest of the function

diff --git a/ui/src/util/extendHeading.js b/ui/src/util/extendHeading.js
index 1234567..abcdefg 100644
--- a/ui/src/util/extendHeading.js
+++ b/ui/src/util/extendHeading.js
@@ -1,15 +1,20 @@
 import { h } from 'vue'
 
 export default function extendHeading (h, level, text, noHtml) {
-  const tag = `h${level}`
-  
-  if (noHtml) {
-    return h(tag, text)
-  }
-  
-  // Vulnerable: text is rendered as HTML without sanitization
-  return h(tag, {
-    innerHTML: text
-  })
+  const tag = `h${level}`
+  
+  if (noHtml) {
+    // When noHtml is set, use textContent to prevent XSS
+    return h(tag, {
+      textContent: text
+    })
+  }
+  
+  // Always use textContent to prevent XSS, even when noHtml is false
+  // This ensures user-controlled header content cannot contain malicious scripts
+  return h(tag, {
+    textContent: text
+  })
 }

diff --git a/core/htmldataprocessor.js b/core/htmldataprocessor.js
index 1234567..abcdefg 100644
--- a/core/htmldataprocessor.js
+++ b/core/htmldataprocessor.js
@@ -1,6 +1,7 @@
 /**
  * @license Copyright (c) 2003-2022, CKSource Holding sp. z o.o. All rights reserved.
- * For licensing, see LICENSE.md or https://ckeditor.com/legal/ckeditor-oss-license
+ * For licensing, see LICENSE.md or https://ckeditor.com/legal/ckeditor-oss-license
+ * CVE-2022-24728 fix: Enhanced HTML attribute validation
  */
 
 ( function() {
@@ -145,6 +146,18 @@
 	 * @param {CKEDITOR.htmlParser.element} element
 	 */
 	function filterElement( element ) {
+		// CVE-2022-24728: Validate and sanitize dangerous attributes
+		if ( element.attributes ) {
+			var dangerousAttrs = [
+				'onabort', 'onactivate', 'onafterprint', 'onafterupdate',
+				'onbeforeactivate', 'onbeforecopy', 'onbeforecut',
+				'onbeforedeactivate', 'onbeforeeditfocus', 'onbeforepaste',
+				'onbeforeprint', 'onbeforeunload', 'onbeforeupdate',
+				'onblur', 'onbounce', 'oncellchange', 'onchange',
+				'onclick', 'oncontextmenu', 'oncontrolselect', 'oncopy',
+				'oncut', 'ondataavailable', 'ondatasetchanged', 'ondatasetcomplete',
+				'ondblclick', 'ondeactivate', 'ondrag', 'ondragend',
+				'ondragenter', 'ondragleave', 'ondragover', 'ondragstart',
+				'ondrop', 'onerror', 'onerrorupdate', 'onfilterchange',
+				'onfinish', 'onfocus', 'onfocusin', 'onfocusout',
+				'onhashchange', 'onhelp', 'oninput', 'onkeydown',
+				'onkeypress', 'onkeyup', 'onlayoutcomplete', 'onload',
+				'onlosecapture', 'onmessage', 'onmousedown', 'onmouseenter',
+				'onmouseleave', 'onmousemove', 'onmouseout', 'onmouseover',
+				'onmouseup', 'onmousewheel', 'onmove', 'onmoveend',
+				'onmovestart', 'onoffline', 'ononline', 'onpagehide',
+				'onpageshow', 'onpaste', 'onpopstate', 'onprogress',
+				'onpropertychange', 'onreadystatechange', 'onreset',
+				'onresize', 'onresizeend', 'onresizestart', 'onrowenter',
+				'onrowexit', 'onrowsdelete', 'onrowsinserted', 'onscroll',
+				'onsearch', 'onselect', 'onselectionchange', 'onselectstart',
+				'onstart', 'onstop', 'onstorage', 'onsubmit', 'ontimeupdate',
+				'onunload', 'onvolumechange', 'onwaiting', 'onwheel'
+			];
+
+			for ( var attrName in element.attributes ) {
+				if ( element.attributes.hasOwnProperty( attrName ) ) {
+					var lowerAttrName = attrName.toLowerCase();
+					// Remove event handler attributes
+					if ( lowerAttrName.indexOf( 'on' ) === 0 ) {
+						delete element.attributes[ attrName ];
+					}
+					// Remove javascript: protocol in href/src
+					if ( ( lowerAttrName === 'href' || lowerAttrName === 'src' ) &&
+						element.attributes[ attrName ] &&
+						element.attributes[ attrName ].toLowerCase().indexOf( 'javascript:' ) === 0 ) {
+						delete element.attributes[ attrName ];
+					}
+				}
+			}
+		}
+
 		// ... rest of the function
 	}

--- a/src/hermes/commands/cli.py
+++ b/src/hermes/commands/cli.py
@@ -60,6 +60,16 @@ def log_options(options):
     """Log the provided options."""
     for key, value in options.items():
         logger.info(f"Option: {key}={value}")
+
+
+def mask_sensitive_value(value):
+    """Mask sensitive values for logging purposes to prevent credential exposure."""
+    if value is None:
+        return None
+    value_str = str(value)
+    if len(value_str) > 4:
+        return value_str[:4] + '*' * (len(value_str) - 4)
+    return '*' * len(value_str)
 
 
 def cli():
@@ -63,7 +73,11 @@ def cli():
     """
     parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
     parser.add_argument('-O', '--option', action='append', dest='options', default=[])
     args = parser.parse_args()
     
     # Log options (masked for security)
     if args.options:
         options_dict = {}
         for opt in args.options:
             key, value = opt.split('=', 1)
             options_dict[key] = value
-        log_options(options_dict)
+        masked_options = {k: mask_sensitive_value(v) for k, v in options_dict.items()}
+        log_options(masked_options)

--- a/fastapi_sso/sso/base.py
+++ b/fastapi_sso/sso/base.py
@@ -1,6 +1,7 @@
 import secrets
 from typing import Optional, Dict, Any
 from fastapi import Request
+from fastapi_sso.exceptions import SSOError
 
 
 class SSOBase:
@@ -15,6 +16,7 @@ class SSOBase:
     def get_login_url(self, request: Request) -> str:
         """Generate login URL with state parameter."""
         state = secrets.token_urlsafe(32)
+        request.session["sso_state"] = state
         return self._build_login_url(state)
 
     def verify_and_process(self, request: Request, state: str) -> None:
@@ -22,6 +24,12 @@ class SSOBase:
         """Verify state and process authentication."""
         # Verify state parameter to prevent CSRF
         stored_state = request.session.get("sso_state")
         if not stored_state or stored_state != state:
             raise SSOError("Invalid state parameter - possible CSRF attack")
         # Clear the state from session after verification
         request.session.pop("sso_state", None)
         # Continue with OAuth callback processing
         self._process_callback(request)

diff --git a/lib/stimulus_reflex/reflex_factory.rb b/lib/stimulus_reflex/reflex_factory.rb
index 1234567..abcdefg 100644
--- a/lib/stimulus_reflex/reflex_factory.rb
+++ b/lib/stimulus_reflex/reflex_factory.rb
@@ -1,6 +1,25 @@
 module StimulusReflex
   class ReflexFactory
+    # Methods that should never be callable on reflex instances
+    # These are dangerous methods that could lead to arbitrary code execution
+    DANGEROUS_METHODS = %i[
+      instance_variable_set
+      instance_variable_get
+      instance_eval
+      class_eval
+      public_send
+      send
+      method
+      __send__
+      system
+      `
+      eval
+      require
+      require_relative
+      load
+      remote_byebug
+      pry
+    ].freeze
 
     def self.create(reflex_class, session, action, params)
       reflex = reflex_class.new(session, action, params)
+      validate_method!(reflex, action)
       reflex
     end
 
+    def self.validate_method!(reflex, method_name)
+      method_sym = method_name.to_sym
+
+      # Check if method is in the dangerous methods list
+      if DANGEROUS_METHODS.include?(method_sym)
+        raise ArgumentError, "Method '#{method_name}' is not allowed on reflex instances"
+      end
+
+      # Check if method is defined on the reflex class or its ancestors (excluding StimulusReflex::Reflex)
+      ancestors = reflex.class.ancestors
+      reflex_index = ancestors.index(StimulusReflex::Reflex)
+      if reflex_index
+        ancestors = ancestors[0...reflex_index]
+      end
+
+      allowed = ancestors.any? do |ancestor|
+        ancestor.public_instance_methods(false).include?(method_sym)
+      end
+
+      unless allowed
+        raise ArgumentError, "Reflex method '#{method_name}' is not defined on class '#{reflex.class.name}' or on any of its ancestors"
+      end
+    end
+
     def self.create_from_target(target, session, params)
       class_name, method_name = target.split('#')
       reflex_class = class_name.constantize
       create(reflex_class, session, method_name, params)
     end
   end
 end
diff --git a/test/reflex_factory_test.rb b/test/reflex_factory_test.rb
index 1234567..abcdefg 100644
--- a/test/reflex_factory_test.rb
+++ b/test/reflex_factory_test.rb
@@ -1,6 +1,25 @@
 require 'test_helper'
 
 class ReflexFactoryTest < ActiveSupport::TestCase
+  test 'should reject dangerous methods' do
+    dangerous_methods = [
+      'instance_variable_set',
+      'instance_eval',
+      'system',
+      'public_send',
+      'send',
+      'eval'
+    ]
+
+    dangerous_methods.each do |method_name|
+      assert_raises(ArgumentError) do
+        StimulusReflex::ReflexFactory.validate_method!(ChatReflex.new(nil, method_name, {}), method_name)
+      end
+    end
+  end
+
+  test 'should reject methods not defined on reflex class' do
+    assert_raises(ArgumentError) do
+      StimulusReflex::ReflexFactory.validate_method!(ChatReflex.new(nil, 'nonexistent_method', {}), 'nonexistent_method')
+    end
+  end
+
   test 'should create reflex instance' do
     reflex = StimulusReflex::ReflexFactory.create(ChatReflex, nil, 'update', {})
     assert_instance_of ChatReflex, reflex

The fix would add a `parameterLimit` option to the qs.parse call.

Let me create a realistic patch:

diff --git a/src/transformers/models/marian/tokenization_marian.py b/src/transformers/models/marian/tokenization_marian.py
index 1234567..abcdefg 100644
--- a/src/transformers/models/marian/tokenization_marian.py
+++ b/src/transformers/models/marian/tokenization_marian.py
@@ -1,6 +1,7 @@
 import re
 import os
 import json
+import time
 from typing import List, Optional, Tuple, Union
 from ...tokenization_utils import PreTrainedTokenizer
 from ...tokenization_utils_base import BatchEncoding
@@ -15,6 +16,14 @@ class MarianTokenizer(PreTrainedTokenizer):
     def __init__(
         self,
         vocab_file: Optional[str] = None,
+        max_pattern_length: int = 1000,
+        regex_timeout: float = 0.1,
         **kwargs
     ):
         super().__init__(**kwargs)
+        self.max_pattern_length = max_pattern_length
+        self.regex_timeout = regex_timeout
+
+    def _validate_regex_pattern(self, pattern: str) -> bool:
+        """Validate regex pattern to prevent ReDoS attacks."""
+        if len(pattern) > self.max_pattern_length:
+            raise ValueError(f"Regex pattern exceeds maximum length of {self.max_pattern_length}")
+        return True
+
     @property
     def vocab_file(self):
         return self._vocab_file
@@ -45,7 +54,12 @@ class MarianTokenizer(PreTrainedTokenizer):
         if pattern is not None:
             try:
                 # Validate pattern before use to prevent ReDoS
-                compiled_pattern = re.compile(pattern)
+                if not self._validate_regex_pattern(pattern):
+                    raise ValueError("Invalid regex pattern")
+                compiled_pattern = re.compile(pattern)
+                # Use timeout mechanism for regex operations
+                start_time = time.time()
+                match = compiled_pattern.search(text)
+                if time.time() - start_time > self.regex_timeout:
+                    raise TimeoutError("Regex operation exceeded timeout")
             except re.error as e:
                 raise ValueError(f"Invalid regex pattern: {e}")
             return match

diff --git a/src/mrkdwn/escape.ts b/src/mrkdwn/escape.ts
index 1234567..abcdefg 100644
--- a/src/mrkdwn/escape.ts
+++ b/src/mrkdwn/escape.ts
@@ -1,6 +1,7 @@
 export function escapeBlockquote(text: string): string {
-  // Vulnerable regex that can cause ReDoS with multibyte characters
-  return text.replace(/[\s\S]*?/g, (match) => {
+  // Fixed regex to prevent catastrophic backtracking with multibyte characters
+  // Using atomic grouping equivalent to prevent excessive backtracking
+  return text.replace(/(?:[\s\S])/g, (match) => {
     // Escape special characters
     return match
       .replace(/&/g, '&')
       .replace(/</g, '<')
       .replace(/>/g, '>')
       .replace(/`/g, '`')
       .replace(/_/g, '_')
       .replace(/\*/g, '*')
       .replace(/\~/g, '~')
       .replace(/\|/g, '|')
       .replace(/=/g, '=')
       .replace(/-/g, '-')
       .replace(/\+/g, '+')
       .replace(/\(/g, '(')
       .replace(/\)/g, ')')
       .replace(/\[/g, '[')
       .replace(/\]/g, ']')
       .replace(/\{/g, '{')
       .replace(/\}/g, '}')
       .replace(/#/g, '#')
       .replace(/\$/g, '$')
       .replace(/%/g, '%')
       .replace(/\^/g, '^')
       .replace(/@/g, '@')
       .replace(/!/g, '!')
       .replace(/"/g, '"')
       .replace(/'/g, ''')
       .replace(/\//g, '/')
       .replace(/\\/g, '\')
       .replace(/\:/g, ':')
       .replace(/;/g, ';')
       .replace(/\*/g, '*')
       .replace(/\+/g, '+')
       .replace(/\=/g, '=')
       .replace(/\-/g, '-')
       .replace(/\|/g, '|')
       .replace(/\./g, '.')
       .replace(/\,/g, ',')
       .replace(/\?/g, '?')
       .replace(/\!/g, '!')
       .replace(/\:/g, ':')
       .replace(/\;/g, ';')
       .replace(/\(/g, '(')
       .replace(/\)/g, ')')
       .replace(/\[/g, '[')
       .replace(/\]/g, ']')
       .replace(/\{/g, '{')
       .replace(/\}/g, '}')
       .replace(/\#/g, '#')
       .replace(/\$/g, '$')
       .replace(/%/g, '%')
       .replace(/\^/g, '^')
       .replace(/@/g, '@')
       .replace(/!/g, '!')
       .replace(/"/g, '"')
       .replace(/'/g, ''')
       .replace(/\//g, '/')
       .replace(/\\/g, '\')
   })
 }

--- a/lib/sync.js
+++ b/lib/sync.js
@@ -1,6 +1,20 @@
 'use strict'
 
+const ALLOWED_ALGOS = new Set([
+  'md5',
+  'sha1',
+  'sha224',
+  'sha256',
+  'sha384',
+  'sha512',
+  'rmd160',
+  'ripemd160',
+])
+
 function pbkdf2Sync(password, salt, iterations, keylen, algo) {
+  // Validate algorithm parameter
+  if (typeof algo !== 'string') {
+    throw new TypeError('algorithm must be a string')
+  }
+  
+  // Normalize algorithm name to lowercase and validate
+  const normalizedAlgo = algo.toLowerCase()
+  if (!ALLOWED_ALGOS.has(normalizedAlgo)) {
+    throw new Error(`Unsupported algorithm: ${algo}`)
+  }
+  
   // ... rest of the function remains unchanged
 }
